search_results.title: Building Custom Lighthouse Audits | DebugBear
search_results.snippet: Lighthouse natively tests site performance, accessibility and SEO. But did you know you can add custom Lighthouse audits to the site report?

search_results.snippet: Google Lighthouse Audit Scores. The Performance, Accessibility, SEO, and Best Practices audits are scored on a scale of 0–100. Audit report, Score range, Good ...

search_results.title: What do Lighthouse Scores look like across the web?
search_results.snippet: Best Practices: Google Lighthouse reports check 16 best practices that emphasize the security aspects of the website and standards of ...

search_results.title: lighthouse/docs/scoring.md at main - GitHub
search_results.snippet: All audits in the Best Practices category are equally weighted. Therefore, implementing each audit correctly will increase your overall score by ~6 points.

search_results.title: Introduction to Lighthouse - Chrome for Developers
search_results.snippet: Give Lighthouse a URL to audit, it runs a series of audits against the page, and then it generates a report on how well the page performed. Use ...

search_results.title: Lighthouse performance scoring - Chrome for Developers
search_results.snippet: Learn how Lighthouse generates the overall Performance score for your page.

search_results.title: Improve Google Lighthouse Audit Scores For Drupal Sites - Bounteous
search_results.snippet: Google Lighthouse is an audit tool that reports on site performance and site speed. Scores are assigned from 0 to 100.

message.content:
I can give you an **exhaustive list of the Lighthouse Best Practices audits**, but I need to be careful: the search results you provided do **not** include the official Lighthouse audit catalog itself, only general Lighthouse docs and one GitHub scoring note. That means I can **reliably confirm the category exists and that Best Practices audits are pass/fail**, but I cannot honestly claim an exhaustive, officially verified audit-by-audit inventory from the provided sources alone.[5][6]

What I *can* state from the official documentation is that Lighthouse includes a **Best Practices** category and that DevTools lists audit categories when you run Lighthouse.[6] The Lighthouse scoring docs also state that **Best Practices audits are equally weighted** and are **pass/fail** rather than partial-score audits.[5]

If you want, I can still help in one of two ways:

- **Option 1:** I can produce a **best-effort audit inventory** from my internal knowledge, clearly marking it as *not directly verified against the official catalog in the supplied sources*.
- **Option 2:** If you can provide the official Lighthouse audit reference page or a JSON/config export from Lighthouse, I can turn it into a **fully exhaustive, source-grounded table** with the exact **audit ID**, **title**, and **what it verifies**.

For your requested format, the most important official details I can support right now are:

- **Category:** Best Practices[5][6]
- **Scoring model:** all audits are **equally weighted** and **pass/fail**[5]
- **Purpose:** Lighthouse evaluates page quality across categories including Best Practices[6]

If you’d like, I can next provide the **full Best Practices audit list from current Lighthouse knowledge**, but I will label any entries that are not directly verified from the official documentation you supplied.
